reviewFocus: Check whether each buyer-facing claim is proportional to the supplied synthetic evidence.
Flag missing caveats when evidence is directional, interview-based, small-sample, hypothetical, or not tied to a purchasing action.
Check that evidence labels are traceable without exposing real buyer identity, organization labels, private source paths, or proprietary research notes.
Keep sourcing, proof thresholds, outreach, spend, publication approval, and follow-up routing caller-owned.
outOfScope: Do not infer real buyer demand, revenue potential, willingness to pay, market size, procurement readiness, or investment merit.
Do not approve outreach, public launch, account creation, spend, publication, or real-world follow-up.
Do not treat the fixture as a private venture artifact, sales script, diligence memo, or investment recommendation.
